verses.text: These are more of Solomon's proverbs, copied by Hezekiah's men; 'it is the glory of God to conceal a thing: but the honour of kings is to search out a matter'; the heart of kings is unsearchable. God hides; kings investigate. The depths of a ruler's mind are beyond knowing.

These are more of Solomon's proverbs, copied by Hezekiah's men; 'it is the glory of God to conceal a thing: but the honour of kings is to search out a matter'; the heart of kings is unsearchable. God hides; kings investigate. The depths of a ruler's mind are beyond knowing.

These are more of Solomon's proverbs, copied by Hezekiah's men; 'it is the glory of God to conceal a thing: but the honour of kings is to search out a matter'; the heart of kings is unsearchable. God hides; kings investigate. The depths of a ruler's mind are beyond knowing.

Take dross from silver and the wicked from before the king, and his throne is established in righteousness; 'put not forth thyself in the presence of the king,' but wait to be called up. Purge evil for stable rule. Humility waits to be exalted rather than pushing forward.

Take dross from silver and the wicked from before the king, and his throne is established in righteousness; 'put not forth thyself in the presence of the king,' but wait to be called up. Purge evil for stable rule. Humility waits to be exalted rather than pushing forward.

Take dross from silver and the wicked from before the king, and his throne is established in righteousness; 'put not forth thyself in the presence of the king,' but wait to be called up. Purge evil for stable rule. Humility waits to be exalted rather than pushing forward.

Go not hastily to strife, lest you know not what to do at the end; debate your cause with your neighbour himself, and reveal not another's secret, lest you bear lasting shame. Settle quarrels privately. Betrayed confidences bring a reproach that clings.

Go not hastily to strife, lest you know not what to do at the end; debate your cause with your neighbour himself, and reveal not another's secret, lest you bear lasting shame. Settle quarrels privately. Betrayed confidences bring a reproach that clings.

Go not hastily to strife, lest you know not what to do at the end; debate your cause with your neighbour himself, and reveal not another's secret, lest you bear lasting shame. Settle quarrels privately. Betrayed confidences bring a reproach that clings.

'A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in pictures of silver'; a wise reprover on an obedient ear is a precious ornament; a faithful messenger refreshes like cold snow in harvest. Timely words are treasures. A trustworthy servant revives those who send him.

'A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in pictures of silver'; a wise reprover on an obedient ear is a precious ornament; a faithful messenger refreshes like cold snow in harvest. Timely words are treasures. A trustworthy servant revives those who send him.

'A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in pictures of silver'; a wise reprover on an obedient ear is a precious ornament; a faithful messenger refreshes like cold snow in harvest. Timely words are treasures. A trustworthy servant revives those who send him.

Whoso boasts of a false gift is like clouds without rain; by patience a ruler is persuaded, and a soft tongue breaks the bone; 'hast thou found honey? eat so much as is sufficient.' Empty promises disappoint. Even good things, taken to excess, become harmful.

Let your foot be seldom in your neighbour's house, lest he weary of you; a false witness is a maul, sword, and arrow; confidence in an unfaithful man in trouble is 'a broken tooth, and a foot out of joint.' Do not overstay welcome. The treacherous fail you when most needed.

Singing songs to a heavy heart is like vinegar on soda; 'if thine enemy be hungry, give him bread,' for so you heap coals of fire on his head, and the LORD will reward you. Kindness conquers. To feed a foe is to overcome evil with good.

The north wind drives away rain, and a backbiting tongue an angry face; better to dwell in a housetop corner than with a brawling woman; 'good news from a far country' is as cold water to a thirsty soul. Strife wearies. Welcome news refreshes the longing heart.

A righteous man falling before the wicked is a troubled fountain; it is not good to eat much honey or seek one's own glory; 'he that hath no rule over his own spirit is like a city... broken down, and without walls.' Self-control is vital. The man who cannot govern himself is defenceless.
